hero.title: 3 Days Yogyakarta
hero.intro: Discover the cultural heartbeat of Java in just three days — from the world's greatest Buddhist temple at Borobudur to an adrenaline-pumping 4WD jeep ride on the slopes of active Mount Merapi. This best-deal package packs iconic sights, royal history, and coastal beauty into a perfectly paced private tour from as little as RM988 per person.

overview.heading: 3 Days of Temples, Volcanoes & Coastal Sunsets in Yogyakarta
body: Yogyakarta — or 'Jogja' as Indonesians affectionately call it — is Java's cultural soul. Home to two UNESCO World Heritage Sites and an active volcano, this compact city rewards travellers who want both history and adventure. Our 3-day best-deal package is designed for Malaysian travellers looking to experience the highlights of Yogyakarta without spending a fortune.

On Day 1 you'll shake off the flight with a coastal drive to the dramatic Parangtritis Beach and Parangkusumo Sand Dunes — optional sandboarding available. Day 2 brings the headline acts: Borobudur, the world's largest Buddhist monument, followed by an exhilarating 4WD jeep tour up Mount Merapi's lava fields. Your final morning wraps up with a regal tour of the Sultan's Palace and the magical Taman Sari Water Castle before your airport transfer. All tours are 100% private — your own guide and vehicle, no strangers.

items.title: Borobudur — Largest Buddhist Temple in the World
items.body: Built in the 9th century by the Sailendra dynasty, Borobudur is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and the single most visited monument in Indonesia. Walk through its nine stacked platforms adorned with 2,672 relief panels and 504 Buddha statues, with sweeping views of the surrounding volcanic landscape at sunrise.
items.imageAlt: Borobudur temple stupa at sunrise, Yogyakarta

items.title: Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour
items.body: Strap into a rugged 4WD jeep and venture up the slopes of one of the world's most active volcanoes. Stop at the Kaliadem bunker — buried by the 2010 eruption — and the Mini Museum of Merapi to understand the raw power beneath your feet. An unforgettable half-day adventure.
items.imageAlt: 4WD jeep on Mount Merapi lava fields, Yogyakarta

items.title: Parangtritis Beach & Parangkusumo Sand Dunes
items.body: Yogyakarta's most famous beach stretches dramatically along the Indian Ocean with dark volcanic sand and rolling waves. The adjacent Parangkusumo Sand Dunes offer optional sandboarding, horse riding, and traditional carriage rides against a backdrop of golden hour skies.

items.title: Gereja Ayam (Chicken Church) Panoramic View
items.body: Perched on a Menoreh Hills ridge near Borobudur, this quirky dove-shaped prayer house is one of Java's most photographed landmarks. Climb to the 'cockscomb' for panoramic views stretching across the Progo River valley and on clear days all the way to Borobudur.
items.imageAlt: Gereja Ayam Chicken Church near Borobudur, Yogyakarta

items.title: Kraton Yogyakarta & Taman Sari Water Castle
items.body: The Kraton is the living royal palace of the Sultan of Yogyakarta, still occupied and rich with Javanese gamelan music, batik artistry and court traditions. A short walk away, Taman Sari — the 18th-century royal garden and bathing complex — reveals hidden pools, underground mosques and a photographer's paradise of Instagrammable archways.
items.imageAlt: Taman Sari Water Castle royal pool, Yogyakarta

days.title: Arrive Yogyakarta — Obelix Sea View, Parangkusumo Sand Dunes & Parangtritis Beach
days.description: Your English-speaking driver-guide will meet you at Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) with a welcome sign. After a comfortable lunch at a local restaurant, head south towards the Indian Ocean coast. First stop is the Obelix Sea View café and viewpoint perched on the Menoreh Hills, offering sweeping panoramas over rice paddies and the distant coastline. Next, tackle the Parangkusumo Sand Dunes where optional sandboarding and ATV rides add a dash of adventure (own expense). The iconic Parangtritis Beach stretches for kilometres of dark volcanic sand — watch the sun drop into the Indian Ocean while optional horse riding and traditional dokar carriage rides pass by. After a satisfying dinner at a beachside warung, check into your hotel in central Yogyakarta for a well-earned rest.
suggestedActivities: Horse riding or dokar carriage ride at Parangtritis Beach (own expense)
Sunset photography at Obelix Sea View
Sample fresh seafood at a coastal warung

days.title: Borobudur Temple — Gereja Ayam (Chicken Church) — Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour — Malioboro Street
days.description: Rise early for a hearty hotel breakfast before setting off for Borobudur, the world's largest Buddhist temple and a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1991. Spend a couple of hours exploring its nine tiered platforms decorated with over 2,672 bas-relief panels and 504 Buddha statues — your guide will bring the 9th-century Sailendra history to life. Afterwards visit the nearby Gereja Ayam (Chicken Church), a striking dove-shaped building on a wooded hilltop near Magelang, with fantastic valley views. Enjoy lunch before heading back towards the city for the afternoon's star attraction: the Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour. Climb into an open 4WD jeep and roar up the volcanic slopes of one of Indonesia's most active volcanoes, stopping at the haunting Kaliadem bunker buried by the 2010 pyroclastic flow, the Batu Alien lava rock formation, and the Mini Museum of Merapi. Cap the evening with shopping along the legendary Malioboro Street — Yogyakarta's iconic batik, silver and wayang puppet market — before returning to the hotel for dinner.
suggestedActivities: Early morning visit to Borobudur for fewer crowds
Climb to the top terrace of Borobudur for 360° volcano views
Photo stop at Gereja Ayam's cockscomb for panoramic valley shots
Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour with bunker visit
Batik and silver shopping on Malioboro Street

days.title: Bakpia Shopping — Sultan's Palace (Kraton) — Taman Sari Water Castle — Departure
days.description: After breakfast, make a quick stop at Bakpia Jogkem, the most famous shop for Jogja's beloved bakpia pastries — the perfect edible souvenir to bring home for family and friends. Then explore the Kraton Yogyakarta, the walled city-within-a-city that serves as the official residence of the Sultan and the cultural centre of Javanese civilisation. Stroll through its open-air pavilions, watch court dancers rehearse, and admire the royal gamelan instruments. A short walk leads to Taman Sari (Water Castle), the 18th-century royal pleasure garden with its iconic bathing pools, underground mosque, and labyrinthine batik-lined alleyways. Enjoy a final lunch before your private vehicle whisks you to Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) for your flight home, full of memories from Java's cultural capital.
suggestedActivities: Stock up on bakpia and local snacks at Bakpia Jogkem
Hire a Kraton guide for deeper royal history insights
Explore the underground passages of Taman Sari
Browse the batik artists' kampung behind Taman Sari

sections.heading: Yogyakarta Tour Package from Malaysia — 3 Days Best Deal
sections.body: Yogyakarta is one of the most popular short-break destinations for Malaysian travellers, and it's easy to see why. Just a 2-hour flight from Kuala Lumpur, this Javanese cultural capital offers a remarkable blend of UNESCO-listed temples, active volcanoes, royal palaces, and vibrant street life — all at Indonesian prices that remain very affordable for Malaysians. Our 3 Days Yogyakarta Best Deal package is specifically designed for travellers who want to experience the very best of Jogja in a compact, stress-free itinerary. Whether you're flying from KLIA, klia2, or Penang, our team arranges seamless airport-to-hotel transfers on both ends, so you can relax the moment you land. The package is valid through March 2027, and prices start from just RM988 per person — making it one of the most competitive Yogyakarta packages available from Malaysia.

sections.heading: Borobudur UNESCO World Heritage Site — What to Expect
sections.body: Borobudur is the undisputed crown jewel of any Yogyakarta itinerary. This 9th-century Mahayana Buddhist monument was built by the Sailendra dynasty and is recognised as the world's largest Buddhist temple — larger even than Angkor Wat in Cambodia. Declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1991, Borobudur consists of nine stacked platforms (six square and three circular) topped by a central dome. The outer walls are covered with 2,672 relief panels telling the life story of the Buddha and Javanese cosmology, while 504 Buddha statues sit in open-air niches across the terraces. From the top platform on a clear morning, you can see Mount Merapi, Mount Merbabu, and Mount Sumbing rising above the mist-covered Kedu Plain — a view that has inspired pilgrims and travellers for over a thousand years. On our 3-day package, you'll visit Borobudur on Day 2 with a knowledgeable English-speaking guide who will bring the relief carvings and Buddhist philosophy to life.

sections.heading: Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour — Yogyakarta's Most Thrilling Activity
sections.body: No visit to Yogyakarta is complete without a Merapi Jeep Tour. Mount Merapi ('Mountain of Fire') is one of the world's most active stratovolcanoes and last erupted catastrophically in 2010, killing over 350 people and displacing 350,000. Today, the lava fields and destroyed villages on its southern slopes have been preserved as a powerful open-air museum. Your 4WD jeep adventure takes you past the Kaliadem bunker — where two rescue workers tragically perished in the 2010 eruption despite sheltering underground — through lava fields solidified into alien landscapes, past the Batu Alien boulder, and to the Mini Museum of Merapi with its sobering collection of eruption artefacts. The tour typically runs in the late afternoon so you can see the volcanic scenery in the golden hour light before returning to Malioboro Street for dinner and shopping. Merapi is visible from much of Yogyakarta, and on clear evenings its faint plume of smoke serves as a constant reminder that this 'mountain of fire' never fully sleeps.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia from Malaysia?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders enjoy visa-free entry to Indonesia for stays of up to 30 days. No pre-arrival visa application is required — simply present your valid Malaysian passport at immigration upon arrival.

items.question: Which airport do I fly into for Yogyakarta?
items.answer: Yogyakarta is served by Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA), located in Kulon Progo about 45 minutes from the city centre. AirAsia operates direct flights from KLIA2, making it very convenient for Malaysians. Our package includes airport transfers on both arrival and departure days.

items.question: What currency does Indonesia use, and can I use Malaysian Ringgit?
items.answer: Indonesia uses the Indonesian Rupiah (IDR). Malaysian Ringgit is not accepted for daily purchases, so you'll need to exchange currency. Money changers are widely available at airports and in Malioboro Street area. As a guide, RM1 ≈ IDR 3,500 (rates vary). We recommend exchanging some cash before or upon arrival.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: All our Yogyakarta packages are 100% private. You will have your own dedicated English-speaking driver-guide and private vehicle throughout the tour. You will not be grouped with other travellers or follow a fixed group schedule.

items.question: Can I customise the itinerary?
items.answer: Yes. While this package follows a set itinerary, we are happy to adjust timings, swap attractions, or add optional activities based on your interests. Contact our team via WhatsApp at +6019 336 4465 to discuss your preferences.

items.question: What is the best time of year to visit Yogyakarta?
items.answer: Yogyakarta is a year-round destination. The dry season runs from May to October and is generally the most popular time to visit, with clear skies ideal for Borobudur sunrise visits. The wet season (November to April) brings afternoon rain but fewer crowds and lower prices. Our packages are valid year-round, though a high-season surcharge may apply during peak periods.

items.question: Are flights included in the package price?
items.answer: Flights are not included. The package price covers land arrangements only — hotel, meals, transfers, entrance fees, and driver-guide. We can assist with flight recommendations and booking upon request.
